original text:
Why is my Branded Sharing Page not inheriting in my Vidyard group?
Both Vidyard subgroups and GoVideo Enterprise users may inherit the branded sharing page (styling and settings) from the parent group of the organization to which they belong.
In some scenarios, users may find that the sharing page on which their video displays has not assumed the custom branding or settings of the sharing page from the parent group.
This articles outlines some basic steps to troubleshoot steps to resolve the problem.
When navigating to a video sharing page, the player will display on a webpage which does not match the design of the Branded Sharing Page in the Vidyard parent group.
This issue can be caused by a lack of connection to the parent account (feature configuration, settings being overridden, etc.), or by the sharing page being disabled.
In some cases, you may be attempting to view a player that has had the Sharing page setting disabled. Check both the General Player Settings for the specific player, as well as the Default Player Settings to ensure that the player is configured to be watched on a sharing page.
Click on the Delete (trash can) button. This will allow the group (or GoVideo account) to re-inherit the branded sharing page layout from the parent group.
If none of the previous options solve your issue, please contact your account CSM or support@vidyard.com to confirm your account settings.
Why aren't thumbnail images being seen by a recipient on Outlook?

If none of the previous options solve your issue, please contact your account CSM or  to confirm your account settings.

original text:
Go green and keep our island clean. This monthly recycling event takes place every third Saturday. Drop off your recyclables from 9:00 AM - 12:00 PM at the parking lot of Lahaina Cannery Mall.
Items NOT accepted include propane tanks, green waste, furniture, trash, motor oil, hazardous fluids/waste, paint, vehicles. As of July 2016, we can no longer accept cooking oil, household batteries, glasses, hearing aids, and books.
Not sure if your item will be accepted? Call to ask, 877-2524, or email info@cwdhawaii.org.

Reservations required by email: mistycreekfm@yadtel.net.

original text:
Table Purchasers: submit GUEST LIST to Joyce Lau at joyce.lau10@hawaiiantel.net by April 15.
The University of Hawaii Foundation (UHF) receives, receipts, and holds funds raised by the UH Men's Basketball program. UHF is a registered 501(c)(3) charitable organization.
In compliance with NCAA regulations regarding benefits to Prospective Student-Athletes, please advise if your guest is in the 7th to 12th grade as some restrictrions may apply.
Proceeds from banquet will go towards summer school scholarships for Men's Basketball Student-Athletes.

Am I in a waking nightmare? Since when can Americans purchase a deadly poison via email?
This entry was posted in crime, Evil and tagged CDC, poison. Bookmark the permalink.
Call your local police and report this right away and file a police report. Also call the FBI and do the same. You may have been targeted by some type of gang. Do it now.
It may not be a Nigerian banking scam, Russian mail-order brides, cheap Viagra or "replica" Rolex watches, but sadly it's that easy... or it's another scam. Either way, not a good thing.
You can also forward spam emails to: spam@uce.gov an FCC government entity that supposedly tracks down and investigates unsolicited criminal spam email.
I am sure somebody here in my community has the real Cyanora. Live in a heavy Police, farming community. Feel sick to stomach, strange musty smells and nauseas when I visit my sister. Sick for days after with flu like symptoms after visit.

original text:
Hopp is a rooftop beergarden, located high above the burstling city of Amsterdam.
Not sure if you can hop by? Check our Facebook page for current opening hours.
Enjoy the sunset during sultry summer evenings.
Choose from over 30 different craft beers!
Check out our menu for the most delicious dishes.
Try our own craft beer: Gabbe!
Take the elevator to the 8th floor and enjoy the radiant sun, the beautiful view, the extensive beer menu and the tastiest dishes.
Feel like popping by (alone or with fewer than 10 of your friends)? You're always welcome at Hopp Amsterdam! If you're meeting more than 10 of your besties on Monday - Thursday, let us know in advance. E-mail us at envelop@hopp-amsterdam.nl. We do not take reservations for Fridays, Saturdays and Sundays, but everyone is still more than welcome!

original text:
The NYCDOE is committed to ensuring that staff and students who use social media for professional and academic purposes do so in a safe and responsible manner. You should review the NYCDOE's Social Media Guidelines and the Internet Acceptable Use and Safety Policy and share them with your staff. Please note that all staff should be judicious in their professional and personal use of social media, and be mindful that social-media posts may be viewed by individuals who are not the intended recipients. For questions, email Lisa Nielsen.
 To better support your parent coordinator (PC) this school year, you should review and familiarize yourself with the Principal Checklist for Parent Coordinators, which provides links to resources and requirements for PCs. All parent coordinators should receive and read PC Connections, the parent coordinator newsletter that provides essential information for all PCs. If your PC is not receiving the newsletter, they may sign up here.
For questions, contact face@schools.nyc.gov or call (212) 374-4118.
City employees may not accept gifts or "tips" for doing their City jobs, nor may they receive any gifts from anyone doing business with the City, including anyone whose interests may be affected by their actions as City employees.
Superiors may not accept holiday gifts from their City subordinates, either individually or collectively, except gifts of negligible monetary value, i.e., under $10. Superiors may give holiday gifts to their subordinates, as long as the gift is not unusually extravagant.
City employees may not accept offers of free attendance at holiday parties or events sponsored by outside entities, such as those doing business with the City, unless they are given permission to do so in writing by their agency head that their attendance would further a legitimate City purpose related to their job. You need not receive any special permission if you pay for your ticket.
A more complete list of the provisions of the City's Conflicts of Interest Law, applicable to all City employees, is summarized below and can be found by following this link. COIB is committed to the fair and equitable enforcement of the law. If you have questions about these or any other City ethics issue, you may contact the Conflicts of Interest Board for advice at (212) 442-1400. The advice is confidential and it can also be anonymous. Should you have evidence or suspicion of corrupt activity anywhere in City government, you are required to report it immediately to the Inspector General assigned to your agency. Any such reports will be kept strictly confidential.
